reasoning_model_1: The model generates the text content 'Oh, when will these days ever end?' accurately. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations', the model attempts a sigh at 00:00, but it is realized primarily as a breathy pronunciation of the word 'Oh' rather than a distinct non-linguistic exhalation. It conveys the sentiment but lacks a clear, separate non-linguistic element. Regarding 'Pace', the model fails to adhere to the instruction 'very slow pace'. The phrase 'when will these days ever end' (00:01-00:03) is spoken at a moderate, conversational speed, completely ignoring the tempo constraint.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the text content accurately. Regarding 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations', the audio begins with a very distinct, realistic, and prolonged sigh (an exhalation of breath) from 00:00 to 00:01 before the speech begins. This perfectly satisfies the requirement for a non-linguistic vocalization. Regarding 'Pace', the speaker delivers the line from 00:01 to 00:05 with significant elongation of words and pauses (e.g., 'ever... end'), effectively capturing the requested 'very slow pace' and enhancing the weary mood.
model_comparison:
Model 2 is superior in both dimensions. 

For Non-Linguistic Vocalizations, Model 2 generates a clear, standalone sigh (breathing sound) that precedes the speech, which is a textbook example of a non-linguistic vocalization. Model 1 merely says the word 'Oh' with a sighing tone, which is less distinct and impactful.

For Pace, Model 2 strictly follows the 'very slow' instruction, stretching the short sentence over 4-5 seconds with a weary drag. Model 1 speaks at a standard conversational rate, failing to implement the specific style command. Model 2's performance is fully correct, while Model 1 has major issues with pace and partial compliance with the vocalization.
